title: Versioning, Delete Markers, Object Lock, and Restore · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A TypeScript pairing room incrementally builds immutable-retention and recovery runbook, validates one changed runtime input, and keeps earlier HTTP, accessibility, security, stream, AbortSignal, and resource-budget rules active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of immutable-retention and recovery runbook while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, distinguish unversioned, enabled, and suspended versioning and test null versions, overwrite behavior, and lifecycle impact.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, list versions and delete markers, resolve current reads, restore by removing a marker or copying a version, and preserve audit identity.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, apply governance, compliance, legal hold, retention date, and authorized bypass by version with irreversible-risk review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Versioning State Transition
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 7109 during a Node stream backpressure incident. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to narrow versioning state and require version-specific retention and restore evidence; do not treat TypeScript types, a resolved promise, CORS, ETag, or an emulator as stronger proof than it is; then double concurrent Upload instances and preserve aggregate memory and worker budgets. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Versioning State Transition.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for in a strict node 24 and typescript 7 service, distinguish unversioned, enabled, and suspended versioning and test null versions, overwrite behavior, and lifecycle impact. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Versioning State Transition, keep Versioning State Transition connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.text: A workshop TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 7109 during a Node stream backpressure incident. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
